source_jobs.title: Senior Finance Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BULLPEN

Bullpen Talent helps U.S. companies build high-performing remote teams. We specialize in placing experienced professionals across finance, accounting, operations, engineering, and other business functions. We're now building a dedicated finance recruiting practice focused on elite financial modeling talent.








ABOUT THE ROLE

Bullpen Talent is hiring a senior recruiter to build and manage a pipeline of skilled financial modeling professionals in India.



This is not a general recruiting role.



You must already have experience recruiting investment banking, private equity, hedge fund, equity research, valuation, quantitative finance, structured finance, or project finance professionals from India's financial outsourcing industry.

You should already know the major firms, understand the differences between these functions, and be comfortable evaluating candidate backgrounds before they ever reach the client.






RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and maintain a pipeline of top financial modeling talent in India.

 - Recruit candidates from firms such as Acuity, TresVista, Evalueserve, Aranca, Copal Amba, SG Analytics, CRISIL, EXL, JP Morgan, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, BlackRock, and similar organizations and BPO/Outsourcing firms.

 - Screen candidates for technical fit and skills before presenting them.

 - Understand the differences between investment banking, equity research, valuation, credit, project finance, real estate, structured finance, private equity, hedge funds, and FP&A.

 - Conduct recruiter interviews and assess communication, experience, and compensation expectations.

 - Negotiate compensation with candidates.

 - Build long-term relationships with high-quality finance professionals.

 - Maintain accurate candidate notes and pipeline status in Ashby.






REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - 5+ years recruiting finance professionals in India.

 - Previous experience recruiting for investment banking, private equity, hedge funds, valuation, equity research, structured finance, or project finance.

 - Strong understanding of financial modeling roles.

 - Excellent spoken and written English.

 - Experience negotiating compensation with Indian finance professionals.

 - Comfortable working independently.






EMPLOYMENT DETAILS

 - Full-time (40 hours/week)

 - Hours: M-F - Must have at least 3-4 hours of overlap with U.S. Pacific Time

 - Remote (India)

 - Independent contractor position

 - Month-to-month engagement

 - No employee benefits (paid leave, health insurance, retirement, etc.)

 - Compensation paid in USD via Deel.com

source_jobs.title: GTM & Strategic Finance Associate
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: GTM & Strategic Finance Associate
Location: Mexico (Remote)
Job Type: Full-Time




Our client is a fast-growing B2B SaaS company seeking a GTM & Strategic Finance Associate to help leadership make smarter, faster decisions across the business.

This role sits at the intersection of Revenue Operations, Strategic Finance, and Business Analytics. You will analyze how the company generates revenue, identify growth opportunities, improve forecasting accuracy, and turn complex data into clear recommendations for executive leadership.

This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys combining numbers, business judgment, and cross-functional impact in a high-growth environment.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build a deep understanding of the company’s revenue engine across Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and Finance.

 - Analyze funnel performance, pipeline trends, bookings, churn, retention, and unit economics.

 - Create and maintain forecasting models for revenue, growth, and operational planning.

 - Monitor key SaaS metrics such as ARR, MRR, CAC, LTV, conversion rates, and retention.

 - Perform variance analysis and explain business performance vs. plan.

 - Prepare executive-level reports, dashboards, and decision-support materials.

 - Support board and investor reporting with accurate business insights.

 - Evaluate pricing, packaging, headcount, and strategic investment decisions.

 - Partner cross-functionally with GTM, Finance, and Leadership teams.

 - Use AI tools to improve reporting efficiency, automate workflows, and enhance analysis.
   
   


MUST-HAVE REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of experience in Strategic Finance, FP&A, RevOps, BizOps, Consulting, or Analytics.

 - Prior experience in a SaaS or recurring-revenue business model.

 - Strong Excel / Google Sheets modeling skills.

 - Excellent analytical thinking and comfort working with data.

 - Strong communication skills with ability to simplify complex findings.

 - Comfortable in fast-paced, changing environments.

 - Fluent English communication skills.
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVE

 - SQL skills.

 - Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Looker, or similar.

 - Experience with Salesforce, HubSpot, or CRM reporting.

 - Understanding of financial statements and SaaS accounting metrics.

 - Experience preparing board or investor materials.

 - Familiarity with AI tools for business productivity.
   
   


WHY JOIN

 - Competitive compensation in USD.

 - Fully remote role based in Mexico.

 - High visibility with leadership and direct business impact.

 - Strong learning and growth opportunity within a scaling SaaS company.

source_jobs.title: Fractional Senior Full Stack Engineer (Stabilization / AI-Generated Code)
source_jobs.description:
FRACTIONAL SENIOR FULL STACK ENGINEER (STABILIZATION / AI-GENERATED CODE)

Location: Remote (LATAM & Eastern Europe)
Type: Contract / Fractional (10–40 hrs/week)




OVERVIEW

We’re hiring a senior, hands-on engineer to support multiple early-stage products that need stabilization.

Most projects were built quickly by non-technical founders using AI tools. As a result, codebases are often incomplete, inconsistent, or fragile.

This role is focused on reviewing, fixing, and stabilizing existing systems not just building new features.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Review and assess existing codebases (often AI-generated or founder-built)

 - Identify bugs, structural issues, and technical risks

 - Refactor and stabilize applications for production use

 - Improve performance, structure, and maintainability

 - Fill gaps in core functionality (auth, payments, backend logic)

 - Implement lightweight QA / testing where needed

 - Collaborate with engineers, QA, and project leads

 - Work across multiple projects as priorities shift


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of full stack experience

 - Strong experience working in messy / inherited codebases

 - Comfortable operating without full documentation

 - Proven ability to take products from MVP → stable product

 - Practical, solution-oriented mindset (not theoretical)

 - Strong communication and team collaboration

 - Comfortable in fast-moving, delivery-focused environments


TECH STACK (FLEXIBLE)

 - Python (FastAPI, Django) - preferred

 - Node.js / TypeScript

 - React / Next.js

 - Firebase, Supabase, or similar

 - APIs & modern web architectures

Flexibility > specialization




ENGAGEMENT MODEL

 - Fractional, hourly

 - 10–40 hrs/week depending on project load

 - Initial engagements ~4 weeks

 - Ongoing work across multiple client projects

source_jobs.title: Financial Modeler
source_jobs.description:
Role: Contract Financial Modeler (Excel)

Hours: 15–40 hours/week · Flexible, asynchronous work

 
 


OVERVIEW

We are partnering with a U.S.-based technology company building advanced AI systems focused on financial modeling and decision-making.

 
 

The goal is to train AI to think like real financial professionals — how models are built from scratch, how assumptions are structured, and how decisions are made using financial models.

 
 

This is a hands-on Excel modeling role. You will be expected to build detailed financial models from a blank spreadsheet and clearly explain your logic.

 
 

We prioritize strong model builders over specific domain experience. Candidates from investment banking, private equity, credit, or corporate finance are encouraged to apply if they have built full models from scratch.

 
 

This role IS:

 - Building financial models from a blank spreadsheet

 - Structuring assumptions, drivers, and logic

 - Linking full model flows (cash flow, debt, returns, scenarios)

 - Debugging and improving model accuracy

 - Explaining why a model works, not just producing outputs

 
 

This role is NOT:

 - Dashboards, reporting, or data visualization

 - Traditional FP&A roles focused primarily on reporting, budgeting, or dashboards without hands-on financial model building

 - Reviewing credit without building models

 - High-level strategy without hands-on modeling

 - Using AI tools to generate models

 
 

What Good Looks Like

Strong candidates have built full financial models end-to-end (e.g., LBO, 3-statement, credit, or project finance models) and can clearly explain every component of the model.

 
 

Areas of Modeling Experience

You must have deep, hands-on experience building full financial models from scratch in at least one of the following areas. We value strong model builders who can adapt across domains.

 
 

Credit & Lending Models

- 3-statement borrower models

- Cash flow-based lending and debt sizing

- Covenant modeling (DSCR, leverage, coverage ratios)

- Loan structuring and scenario analysis

 
 

Private Equity (LBO Modeling)

- Full LBO models built from scratch

- Sources & uses, debt schedules, returns (IRR/MOIC)

- Operating models with sensitivities and exit scenarios

 
 

Asset-Based Lending (ABL) (optional / less common)

- Borrowing base models (AR, inventory)

- Advance rates, eligibility, dilution

- Collateral-driven lending structures

 
 

Structured Finance / Asset-Backed Securities (ABS) (optional / less common)

- Cash flow waterfall models

- Tranching, credit enhancement, loss assumptions

- Prepayment/default scenario modeling

 
 

Risk & Portfolio Modeling

- Scenario and stress-testing models

- Loss modeling (default, recovery, exposure)

- Portfolio-level analysis driven by model outputs

 
 

Note: Candidates with strong experience in LBO or credit modeling are highly relevant, even without direct ABL or structured finance experience.

 
 

What You’ll Do

Build financial models from scratch in Excel across different use cases

Review and debug models built by others

Identify incorrect assumptions, broken logic, and risks

Analyze relationships across cash flow, debt, and returns

Clearly explain financial logic and decision-making

Translate real-world financial thinking into structured outputs

 
 

What Makes a Strong Candidate

 - 2–10 years of experience in finance roles with heavy modeling exposure

 - Proven ability to build models from a blank spreadsheet (no templates)

 - Experience building decision-driving models (not just outputs or summaries)

 - Strong understanding of how models are used in real financial decisions

 - High attention to detail and model accuracy

 - Strong communication skills (English required)

 
 

Not a Fit If

Your experience is primarily:

 - Reviewing modes, making small updates, focused on pitch decks, comps, or outputs without building full models

 - FP&A reporting, budgeting, or dashboards

 - Credit review

source_jobs.title: Financial Modeler (Real Estate)
source_jobs.description:
Title: Contract Real Estate Financial Modeler (Excel)

Hours: 15–40 hours/week · Flexible, asynchronous work.



The Role

We are seeking finance experts for an hourly AI training role. You will build complex, end-to-end acquisition and development models from a blank spreadsheet to provide "gold standard" logic for advanced AI systems. Your goal is to teach the AI how professional assumptions are structured and how financial decisions are made.



What You’ll Do

 - Build and maintain detailed financial models in Excel for acquisitions and development projects.

 - Perform debt analysis, including DSCR, loan sizing, and capital structure evaluation.
   
   

Requirements



Candidates must have 2–10 years of experience in Real Estate underwriting, valuation and analysis. This role focuses strictly on technical financial modeling and logic, not FP&A reporting, dashboards, or high-level strategy.



This is a remote, flexible position requiring a paid 3-hour Excel modeling test. You must be able to debug logic and explain every link in your model.

source_jobs.title: Assistant Controller
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Assistant Controller

Location: Monterrey, Nuevo León, Mexico

Job Type: Full-Time, Hybrid.




ABOUT THE CLIENT

Our client is a high-growth technology and fintech SaaS company with offices in Monterrey, New York, and Switzerland.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We are seeking a skilled Assistant Controller to support the company’s global accounting operations. This role provides oversight of the general ledger, drives monthly and quarterly closing activities, and prepares internal financial statements. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams —FP&A, Operations, and Revenue— to assist with consolidations, account reconciliations, variance analysis, and internal controls, while contributing directly to process standardization appropriate for a SaaS environment.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Global Payroll: Manage operations across multiple platforms including JustWorks, Remote.com/EOR http://Remote.com/EOR, Deel, CONTPAQi, BVG/Swiss pension, and 1099s.

 - Treasury & Cash: Provide bank reconciliation oversight, manage FX (USD/MXN/CHF), perform Ramp/credit card reviews, and handle intercompany wires.

 - Month-End Close: Execute comprehensive month-end close activities, including accruals, prepaids, and intercompany eliminations.

 - Multinational Compliance: Maintain compliance across Swiss AHV/BVG and Mexico SAT/CFDI; manage intercompany loans and support audit PBCs.

 - Systems & Automation: Support audits and help implement, optimize, and standardize financial systems and processes, including revenue recognition, subscription billing, and deferred revenue tracking.
   
   


MUST-HAVE

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.

 - Prior experience in an Assistant Controller, Senior Accountant, or similar role, preferably within a technology, SaaS, or high-growth company.

 - Strong foundation in accounting and financial reporting, including general ledger management, month-end close, and financial statement preparation under relevant standards (US GAAP or IFRS).

 - Advanced proficiency in spreadsheets and financial analysis tools, with a dedicated focus on SQL, PowerQuery, and Python abilities to manipulate data and drive process efficiency.

 - Excellent collaboration skills, able to present financial data to non-finance stakeholders.
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVE

 - Professional certification (e.g., CPA, CMA, or local equivalent) or progress toward certification.

 - Practical technical experience or awareness of PowerBI, Power Automate, Zapier, and n8n.
   
   


WHY JOIN?

 - Flexible work hours and hybrid work options (combining on-site collaboration with the flexibility to work from home part of the week).

 - Professional development opportunities in a modern, automated financial operations environment.

source_jobs.title: Freelance Developer
source_jobs.description:
About Bullpen Talent

Bullpen Talent connects high-caliber freelancers with US-based startups, agencies, and growing brands looking for remote contract talent. We work across design, development, and creative services — placing freelancers in projects where their skills can make a real impact.

We're continuously building our bench of vetted developers for upcoming client placements. If you do great work, we'd love to hear from you.





What the work looks like

Our clients are typically US-based startups and SMBs who need talented developers who can own projects end to end. Work ranges from web builds and redesigns to custom integrations, automation workflows, and app development. You'll be working directly with founders, marketing leads, and product teams — so clear communication matters as much as clean code.





What we look for

 - 2+ years of professional development experience

 - Experience doing freelance, self managed work

 - Strong portfolio showing real client work

 - Comfortable working independently and managing client relationships

 - Detail-oriented with a reliable approach to delivery and deadlines

 - Available for remote contract engagements





Nice to have:

 - Experience with no-code or low-code tools (Webflow, Framer, Bubble, etc.)

 - Familiarity with automation platforms (Zapier, Make, n8n, etc.)

 - Experience working with US time zones





How it works

We're actively building our bench of developers for ongoing US client placements. Submit your application and portfolio and we'll be in touch when a matching opportunity comes up.



A tool for freelancers who price their own work

If you run your own freelance practice alongside contract work, you might find this useful — Payo is a proposal strategist and pricing tool built specifically for freelancers working with US clients. It catches the scope creep you missed, tells you if you're underpricing, and turns a messy client brief into a polished proposal — before you send something you'll regret.



Free to get started: 👉 trypayo.com http://trypayo.com

source_jobs.title: Outbound Sales Team Manager
source_jobs.description:
OUTBOUND SALES TEAM MANAGER




ABOUT BULLPEN TALENT

Bullpen Talent is a staffing company that connects remote professionals with growing U.S. businesses.

We are hiring an Outbound Sales Team Manager for a Washington-based home renovation company.

Location: Remote (LATAM)

Working Hours: U.S. Pacific Time

Compensation: $1,800 USD per month. Performance-based bonuses may be available after several months based on results.






ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for someone who can build and manage a low-cost outbound sales team focused on generating appointments with local US homeowners.

This role is responsible for the entire outbound process, including hiring agents, training them, sourcing homeowner data, creating outreach processes, managing performance, and improving results.

You will not be the primary caller. Your job is to build and manage the team that does the calling.






RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Recruit, hire, and train outbound sales representatives

 - Build a high-performing team using lower-cost remote talent

 - Ensure agents sound professional and can effectively communicate with U.S. homeowners

 - Create scripts, call flows, and training materials

 - Source homeowner data and prospect lists

 - Identify and manage lead sources and data providers

 - Monitor team performance and appointment generation

 - Coach underperforming agents and improve results

 - Manage schedules, coverage, and daily operations

 - Track KPIs and report results to management






REQUIREMENTS

 - Experience working in home services (roofing, remodeling, restoration, HVAC, solar, windows, landscaping, etc.)

 - Experience managing outbound sales or appointment-setting teams

 - Experience with cold calling and outbound lead generation

 - Experience hiring, training, and managing lower-cost remote teams

 - Experience sourcing homeowner leads and prospect lists

 - Strong sales management and coaching skills

 - Strong English communication skills

 - Available during U.S. business hours






IDEAL CANDIDATE

You have built and managed an outbound sales or appointment-setting team before.

You understand how to find quality homeowner data, hire and train remote agents, maintain quality standards, and consistently generate appointments.

You know how to keep a remote team motivated, accountable, and performing at a high level.

source_jobs.title: Inbound Sales Team Manager
source_jobs.description:
INBOUND SALES TEAM MANAGER

Location: Remote (LATAM)

Working Hours: U.S. Pacific Time

Compensation: $1,800 USD per month. Additional bonuses may be available based on performance.



Bullpen Talent is hiring an Inbound Sales Team Manager for a U.S. home renovation company.

The primary responsibility of this role is to build and manage a team of low-cost remote representatives who handle inbound homeowner inquiries and schedule appointments.

Homeowners contact the company directly to:

 - Request renovation / construction estimates

 - Schedule appointments

 - Reschedule appointments

 - Ask questions about services

 - Follow up on projects

We are looking for someone who has previously built and managed this type of team.






RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Recruit, hire, train, and manage a team of low-cost remote customer service and scheduling representatives

 - Create processes, scripts, and training materials

 - Ensure inbound leads are answered quickly and professionally

 - Monitor team performance and quality

 - Coach underperforming team members

 - Maintain strong appointment booking rates

 - Keep the team motivated, accountable, and productive

 - Report results and recommend improvements






REQUIREMENTS

 - Experience working for a home services company (roofing, remodeling, HVAC, restoration, windows, landscaping, solar, etc.)

 - Experience building and managing teams of remote customer service, scheduling, or appointment-setting representatives

 - Experience recruiting and managing lower-cost remote staff

 - Experience creating processes and training programs

 - Strong English communication skills

 - Available during U.S. business hours

 - Extremely reliable






IDEAL CANDIDATE

You have personally built and managed a remote team responsible for handling inbound homeowner inquiries and scheduling appointments.

You know how to find good people, train them quickly, manage performance, and keep a lower-cost remote team operating at a high level.



Compensation: $1,800 USD per month, plus bonuses after several months of performance.




TO APPLY

Please tell us:

 - What type of home services company you worked for

 - How many people you managed

 - Where the team was located

 - How you recruited the team

 - What results the team achieved

source_jobs.title: Property Management Assistant (US / Remote)
source_jobs.description:
Property Management Assistant (Remote | U.S. Residential Real Estate)

Bullpen Talent recruits exceptional remote professionals for growing U.S. property management companies and real estate owners.

We're building our network of experienced Property Management Assistants for current and upcoming opportunities with clients across the United States.

If you've worked with U.S. residential property managers, landlords, or real estate investors, we'd love to hear from you.



Typical Responsibilities

Responsibilities vary by client but often include:

 - Responding to tenant emails, calls, and maintenance requests

 - Coordinating maintenance vendors and work orders

 - Managing lease renewals and lease documentation

 - Processing rental applications and screening documents

 - Posting rental listings and coordinating showings

 - Assisting with move-ins and move-outs

 - Tracking rent payments and following up on delinquencies

 - Managing resident communications

 - Updating property management software

 - General administrative support for property managers



Property Management Software

Experience with one or more of the following is highly preferred:

 - AppFolio

 - Buildium

 - Rent Manager

 - Propertyware

 - Yardi

 - ResMan

 - TenantCloud

 - DoorLoop

 - MRI

 - Entrata

 - RealPage

 - Other property management software



Requirements

 - Previous experience supporting U.S. residential property management companies

 - Excellent spoken and written English

 - Strong customer service skills

 - Highly organized with excellent attention to detail

 - Comfortable speaking with tenants, vendors, and property owners

 - Reliable computer and high-speed internet

 - Ability to work full-time during U.S. business hours



Nice to Have

 - Experience with Section 8 or affordable housing

 - HOA or condominium management experience

 - Leasing experience

 - Maintenance coordination experience

 - Bookkeeping or property accounting experience

 - Spanish fluency



Compensation

Compensation depends on experience, software knowledge, and responsibilities.



About Bullpen Talent

Bullpen Talent places experienced remote professionals with high-quality U.S. companies. Our contractors work as dedicated members of our clients' teams while enjoying long-term, fully remote opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Cold Caller & Appointment Setter (Remote)
source_jobs.description:
Remote Cold Caller (English) | U.S. Sales Campaigns



Bullpen Talent connects exceptional remote professionals with growing U.S. companies.

We're building our network of experienced Cold Callers for current and upcoming opportunities across multiple industries, including home services, real estate, property management, software, and professional services.

If you're confident making outbound calls, handling objections, and booking qualified appointments, we'd love to hear from you.



What You'll Do

Responsibilities vary by client but typically include:

 - Make high-volume outbound cold calls to prospective customers

 - Qualify leads using client-provided criteria

 - Schedule appointments for sales representatives

 - Follow call scripts while adapting naturally to conversations

 - Handle objections professionally and confidently

 - Update CRM systems with accurate notes and outcomes

 - Follow up with prospects when appropriate

 - Meet daily and weekly activity goals



We're Looking For

 - Previous experience in cold calling, outbound sales, appointment setting, or inside sales

 - Excellent spoken English with a clear, professional phone presence

 - Comfortable making 100+ outbound calls per day

 - Strong listening and objection-handling skills

 - Self-motivated and results-driven

 - Reliable internet connection and quiet home office

 - Available to work during U.S. business hours



Experience With These Tools Is a Plus

 - Salesforce

 - HubSpot

 - GoHighLevel

 - Zoho CRM

 - Pipedrive

 - Close CRM

 - Follow Up Boss

 - Apollo.io http://Apollo.io

 - Sales Navigator

 - Mojo Dialer

 - CallTools

 - RingCentral

 - Aircall

 - Dialpad

 - Five9

 - PhoneBurner

 - Other CRM or dialing software



Industries You've Worked In (Any)

Experience in one or more of the following is a plus:

 - Home Services (HVAC, Plumbing, Roofing, Electrical, Remodeling)

 - Real Estate

 - Property Management

 - Solar

 - Insurance

 - Mortgage

 - SaaS

 - Marketing Agencies

 - Financial Services

 - B2B Sales

 - Healthcare

 - Construction

 - Other outbound sales environments



Compensation

Compensation depends on experience, English proficiency, and the complexity of the campaign. This is a contractor role that is hourly based.



About Bullpen Talent

Bullpen Talent places experienced remote professionals with high-quality U.S. companies. Our contractors become dedicated members of their clients' teams while enjoying long-term, fully remote opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Senior Bookkeeper
source_jobs.description:
Senior Bookkeeper (Remote | Part-Time)



Bullpen Talent is seeking an experienced Senior Bookkeeper to support the financial operations of a growing U.S.-based business.

This is a long-term, part-time contractor role requiring approximately 5–10 hours per week. Workload is generally light but increases around mid-month (customer invoicing) and month-end close, so reliability during these periods is essential.

If you're looking for a stable, ongoing part-time engagement and enjoy keeping financial records accurate and organized, we'd love to hear from you.





Responsibilities

 - Maintain accurate books in QuickBooks Online

 - Reconcile bank accounts, credit cards, and balance sheet accounts

 - Manage accounts payable and vendor payments

 - Prepare and send customer invoices

 - Monitor accounts receivable and follow up on outstanding invoices

 - Process payments through Bill.com http://Bill.com

 - Record payroll and contractor transactions

 - Review transactions for proper coding and classification

 - Assist with monthly financial close

 - Prepare basic financial reports, including Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet

 - Investigate discrepancies and resolve accounting issues

 - Maintain organized financial records and supporting documentation

 - Work directly with management and external CPA as needed





Required Qualifications

 - 5+ years of bookkeeping experience supporting U.S. businesses

 - Advanced QuickBooks Online experience

 - Strong understanding of U.S. bookkeeping principles

 - Experience completing monthly reconciliations independently

 - Experience managing both accounts payable and accounts receivable

 - Excellent attention to detail

 - Strong organizational and communication skills

 - Excellent written and spoken English

 - Reliable computer and high-speed internet





Preferred Experience

Experience with one or more of the following:

 - Bill.com http://Bill.com

 - Deel

 - Gusto

 - Coupa

 - Ramp

 - Microsoft Excel

 - Google Sheets

 - Contractor-heavy businesses

 - Staffing or professional services companies





What We're Looking For

The ideal candidate is:

 - Looking for a long-term part-time role, not a temporary position while seeking full-time employment

 - Dependable and responsive, especially during invoicing and month-end close

 - Able to work independently with minimal supervision

 - Detail-oriented and committed to producing accurate work

 - Proactive in identifying and resolving issues

 - Comfortable communicating directly with management





Position Details

 - Schedule: Approximately 5–10 hours per week

 - Work Arrangement: Fully remote

 - Engagement: Hourly contractor

 - Time Zone: Some overlap with U.S. Pacific Time business hours is preferred

 - Compensation: $16 USD per hour (fixed)





About Bullpen Talent

Bullpen Talent connects exceptional remote professionals with growing U.S. companies. We value long-term relationships, professionalism, and delivering high-quality work for our clients.

source_jobs.title: Senior Financial Modeling Interviewer & Reviewer
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Senior Financial Modeling Interviewer & Reviewer

Location: United Kingdom, Europe (Remote)

Job Type: Part-Time (15-20 hours per week)




ABOUT THE ROLE

Bullpen Talent is supporting a fast-growing, venture-backed AI software company headquartered in San Francisco. The company is scaling quickly and building a global network of finance professionals across investment banking, private equity, corporate finance, FP&A, credit, debt, quant, and other financial modeling disciplines.

We are hiring two part-time finance professionals to help review financial modeling take-home exercises and conduct final-round technical interviews with candidates.

This is not a heavy model-building role. The primary work is reviewing models, evaluating candidate work, conducting interviews, and helping maintain a high technical bar.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Review financial modeling take-home exercises submitted by candidates.

 - Conduct final-round technical interviews with finance candidates.

 - Evaluate candidates’ modeling logic, Excel structure, assumptions, and analytical reasoning.

 - Review answer keys and identify where models are correct, incomplete, or wrong.

 - Explain candidate strengths and weaknesses clearly to the client team.

 - Help assess candidates across finance areas such as DCF, FP&A, LBO, credit, debt, quant, portfolio analysis, and bond/fixed income modeling.

 - Support hiring operations, candidate evaluation, and process improvement as needed.
   
    


IDEAL BACKGROUND

 

We are looking for two complementary profiles:



Profile 1: Broader corporate finance / IB / PE background

 - Investment banking

 - Private equity

 - Corporate finance

 - FP&A

 - Valuation

 - LBO / DCF / operating models

 - 

Profile 2: Debt / credit / quant background

 - Credit analysis

 - Debt capacity

 - Covenants

 - Fixed income / bond pricing

 - Quantitative finance

 - Portfolio analysis

 - Structured finance or risk modeling

You do not need to be an expert in every area, but you must be strong enough to review financial models, understand candidate reasoning, and identify why an answer is right or wrong.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Strong financial modeling background.

 - Experience reviewing, building, or evaluating Excel-based financial models.

 - Excellent spoken and written English.

 - Strong communication skills and professional presence on candidate calls.

 - Comfortable conducting live interviews with candidates.

 - Detail-oriented and able to explain technical feedback clearly.

 - Interest in finance-related recruiting operations, interviewing, and candidate evaluation.

 - Familiarity with US GAAP is helpful.
   
   


WORKING HOURS

 - Remote.

 - Part-time, approximately 15–20 hours per week.

 - Flexible schedule, but this role includes live candidate interviews across India, South Asia, and Latin America.

 - Must be comfortable with time zone flexibility.

 - Some coordination with a U.S.-based client team is also required.

source_jobs.title: Finance Recruiting Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Finance Recruiting Operations Specialist

Location: Worldwide (UK or Europe strongly preferred)

Job Type: Part-time, Remote




ABOUT THE ROLE

Bullpen Talent is supporting a fast-growing, venture-backed AI software company headquartered in San Francisco. The company is scaling quickly and hiring finance professionals globally across investment banking, private equity, FP&A, corporate finance, credit, debt, quant, and financial modeling.

We are hiring a part-time Finance Recruiting Operations Specialist to help manage the candidate funnel, conduct candidate calls, coordinate interviews, and keep the hiring process moving across multiple international time zones.

This role is ideal for someone with both finance recruiting experience and strong candidate-management skills.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Conduct candidate screening calls for finance and financial modeling roles.

 - Manage candidate communication throughout the hiring process.

 - Coordinate interviews across India, South Asia, Latin America, Europe, and the U.S.

 - Keep candidates engaged and informed.

 - Track candidate status, notes, next steps, and follow-ups in the ATS.

 - Help manage take-home exercise logistics and interview scheduling.

 - Work closely with the U.S.-based client team to keep the recruiting process organized.

 - Help improve recruiting operations, process flow, and candidate experience.
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - Experience in recruiting finance professionals.

 - Strong understanding of finance roles, including investment banking, private equity, corporate finance, FP&A, credit, debt, quant, and valuation.

 - Excellent spoken and written English.

 - Very strong communication skills and professional presence.

 - Highly reliable and organized.

 - Able to manage a high-volume candidate pipeline.

 - Comfortable working across multiple time zones.

 - Experience with an ATS or recruiting CRM.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-moving startup environment.
   
   


LOCATION / TIME ZONE

 - Remote.

 - UK or Europe strongly preferred.

 - Must be able to handle candidate calls across both India/South Asia and Latin America.

 - Must also have some overlap with the U.S.-based client team.

 - This role requires time zone flexibility and strong reliability.
   
   


POSITION DETAILS

 - Part-time.

 - Approximately 20–25 hours per week.

 - Contractor role.

 - Remote.

 - Opportunity for a long-term engagement.

notes: Bullpen Talent is a staffing company with a mix of finance, sales, and tech roles. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
